region_of_origin: United States (popularised globally, served in Singapore)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No specific FoodOn term exists for rainbow cake; closest parent 'cake food product' (FOODON_00002403) used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC (Cakes) is the closest parent category; no specific rainbow cake or cream-cheese layer cake code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept for rainbow cake; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard slice of rainbow cake weighing approximately 120 g, as typically served in a café setting in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Cake, white, prepared from recipe with coconut frosting — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy; supplemented with USDA FoodData Central entry for cheesecake (cream cheese layer) to account for cream cheese and mascarpone frosting layers
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Rainbow cake is a multi-coloured vanilla sponge cake with cream cheese and mascarpone frosting layers. No exact USDA or HPB entry exists for this specific product. Values are estimated by blending USDA SR Legacy data for white layer cake (FDC ID 18101) and cream cheese frosting / cheesecake components (FDC ID 18147, 19270). The resulting composite reflects approximately 60% vanilla sponge and 40% cream cheese–mascarpone filling/frosting by weight, consistent with typical café-style rainbow cake construction. Energy and macros are cross-checked against published nutritional analyses of comparable commercial rainbow cakes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — white cake / vanilla sponge cake analogous entry; Atkinson FS, Brand-Miller JC, Foster-Powell K, Buyken AE, Goletzke J. Am J Clin Nutr. 2021
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No GI value exists specifically for rainbow cake. The GI of 65 is an analogous estimate based on white/vanilla sponge cake entries in the University of Sydney GI Database and the 2021 International GI Tables (Atkinson et al., Am J Clin Nutr 2021). White cake with sugar frosting has a reported GI of approximately 65 (medium). The cream cheese and mascarpone layers add fat and protein which may modestly lower the effective GI; however, the dominant carbohydrate matrix is the vanilla sponge, so GI 65 is retained as a conservative estimate. Glycemic load per 120 g serving = (65 × 47.0 × 1.20) / 100 = 36.8.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (210 mg/100 g) and high saturated fat from cream cheese and mascarpone
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit rainbow cake consumption to occasional small portions, as the sodium content (approximately 252 mg per 120 g slice) and saturated fat load from cream cheese and mascarpone frosting can contribute to elevated blood pressure and endothelial stress when consumed regularly.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined sugar content (32 g/100 g) and medium glycemic index (65) producing a high glycemic load per serving (GL ≈ 37)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat rainbow cake as an occasional indulgence only; a standard 120 g slice delivers approximately 38 g of sugar and a glycemic load of ~37, which can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation and should be accounted for within the daily carbohydrate budget.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (10.8 g/100 g), trans fat (0.2 g/100 g), cholesterol (78 mg/100 g), and added sugar
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should minimise rainbow cake intake, as the combination of high saturated fat from cream cheese and mascarpone, trace trans fat, and high added sugar content is associated with raised LDL cholesterol, triglycerides, and systemic inflammation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Rainbow Cake is a visually striking multi-layered vanilla sponge cake in which each sponge layer is dyed a different colour of the rainbow (typically red, orange, yellow, green, blue, and violet) using food-grade colouring. The layers are bound and frosted with a rich mixture of cream cheese and mascarpone, producing a dense, moist crumb with a tangy, lightly sweet frosting. The cake is popular in Western-style cafés and dessert shops across Singapore, where it is commonly sold by the slice. Nutritionally, rainbow cake is energy-dense at approximately 370 kcal per 100 g, with a high sugar content (~32 g/100 g) and significant saturated fat (~10.8 g/100 g) from the dairy-based frosting. The medium glycemic index (~65) combined with a large carbohydrate load per slice results in a high glycemic load (~37 per 120 g serving), making it a high-concern food for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular disease. The cream cheese and mascarpone layers also contribute meaningful calcium (~62 mg/100 g) and vitamin A (~98 mcg RAE/100 g). Rainbow cake contains gluten, dairy, and eggs and is not suitable for vegans or those with these allergens.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a slice of rainbow cake?
Is rainbow cake suitable for diabetics?
What is the sugar content of rainbow cake?
What is rainbow cake made of?
How much fat is in rainbow cake?
What is the GI of rainbow cake?
Can I eat rainbow cake if I have high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for rainbow cake exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy white layer cake and cream cheese/cheesecake component data, blended in proportions consistent with typical café-style rainbow cake construction. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database white cake entry. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for white layer cake (FDC 18101) and cream cheese frosting/cheesecake (FDC 18147, 19270); no exact HPB or USDA entry for rainbow cake exists. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from USDA component data and are not independently verified for this specific product; they are reported as best-available estimates rather than confirmed values. 3. No -1 sentinel values are used in this record; all micronutrient fields carry estimated numeric values derived from approved USDA sources. 4. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate based on white/vanilla sponge cake in the University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al., Am J Clin Nutr 2021); no direct GI measurement for rainbow cake was found. 5. Halal status is assumed based on typical café-style preparation without pork-derived gelatin; actual halal certification for The Tree Cafe - Funan was not verified and should be confirmed. 6. The food_name field strips the decorative star emoji (⭐) present in the input to produce a clean canonical English name. 7. Cuisine tag in input was 'Coffee' (café context); canonical cuisine is recorded as 'Western' consistent with the dish's origin and preparation style.
